channel.title: VPS Guides & Tutorials on VPS Catalog - Find the Best VPS Hosting for Your Needs
channel.description: Recent content in VPS Guides & Tutorials on VPS Catalog - Find the Best VPS Hosting for Your Needs

item.title: Best Cheap VPS Under $5 2026
item.description:
Best Cheap VPS Under $5

You don’t need to spend much to get a decent VPS. Here’s what’s available for under $5/mo:

Provider Price vCPU RAM Storage Bandwidth
Vultr $2.50/mo 1 512 MB 10 GB SSD 500 GB
Hetzner €3.99/mo 2 4 GB 40 GB NVMe 10 TB
RackNerd $3.99/mo 1 1 GB 25 GB SSD 2 TB

🥇 Hetzner CX22 — Best Value at €3.99/mo

For just €3.99/mo you get 2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and 40 GB NVMe storage. That’s more than most $10-12/mo plans from other providers.

item.title: Best Unmanaged VPS Providers 2026
item.description:
Best Unmanaged VPS Providers

Unmanaged VPS is the sweet spot for developers and sysadmins. You get full root access and lower prices, but you handle security, updates, and setup yourself.

Provider Price vCPU RAM Storage Bandwidth
Hetzner €3.99/mo 2 4 GB 40 GB NVMe 10 TB
Vultr $2.50/mo 1 512 MB 10 GB SSD 500 GB
DigitalOcean $6/mo 1 1 GB 25 GB SSD 1 TB
Linode $5/mo 1 1 GB 25 GB SSD 1 TB
Contabo €5.99/mo 4 8 GB 200 GB SSD 32 TB

🥇 Hetzner — Unbeatable for Unmanaged

€3.99/mo for 2 vCPU, 4 GB RAM, NVMe storage, and 10 TB transfer. No other provider comes close at this price. Perfect if you’re comfortable setting up your own LEMP/LAMP stack.

item.title: Best VPS for AI and Machine Learning 2026
item.description:
Best VPS for AI & ML

AI workloads need serious compute. For small to medium projects, cloud VPS with GPU options can be surprisingly affordable.

Provider GPU Option Starting Price Best For
Vultr NVIDIA A100 $2.50/mo (CPU) / GPU from $0.69/hr Budget ML experiments
DigitalOcean Droplet + Paperspace $6/mo (CPU) Development & prototyping
Hetzner Dedicated GPU €3.99/mo (CPU) / GPU from €89/mo Serious training on a budget

By Use Case

Use Case Recommended Why
Experimenting / learning Vultr GPU ($0.69/hr) Pay by the hour, cheap to try
Running inference (small models) DigitalOcean $12/mo Enough for lightweight AI
Training models Hetzner GPU (€89/mo) Best price for dedicated GPU
Large-scale training Dedicated Hetzner Full control, high performance

Quick Tip

For AI work, don’t get locked annual contracts. GPU pricing drops every year. Stick with hourly billing (Vultr) or month-to-month (Hetzner) so you can upgrade when newer GPUs launch.

item.title: Best VPS for Forex Trading 2026
item.description:
Best VPS for Forex Trading

Forex traders need ultra-low latency and 99.99%+ uptime. Your VPS should be in the same data center region as your broker.

Provider Starting Price Key Feature Best Region
Vultr $6/mo 32 data centers, match broker DC Global
DigitalOcean $12/mo Consistent performance NY, London, Frankfurt
Hetzner €3.99/mo Lowest latency EU Nuremberg, Helsinki

What Forex Traders Need

  • Low latency — Every millisecond matters in scalping
  • Proximity to broker — Choose a data center near your broker’s servers
  • 99.99% uptime — Don’t miss trades due to downtime
  • Static IP — Some brokers require whitelisted IPs
  • Windows VPS — Many trading platforms (MT4/MT5) need Windows

Our Pick

Vultr is the best all-around choice for forex traders because of their 32 data centers — you can almost always find one close to your broker. Start with the $6/mo plan and upgrade as needed.

item.title: Best VPS for Minecraft Server 2026
item.description:
Best VPS for Minecraft

Minecraft servers need CPU power and RAM more than anything else. Here’s what works.

Provider Starting Price RAM (minimum) Why
Contabo €5.99/mo 8 GB Most RAM per dollar
Hetzner €6.99/mo 8 GB Best CPU performance
Vultr $12/mo 2 GB Global player coverage

RAM Requirements by Player Count

Players Recommended RAM Example Plan
1-5 2 GB Vultr $6/mo
5-15 4 GB Hetzner CX32
15-30 8 GB Contabo or Hetzner CX42
30-50 16 GB Hetzner AX dedicated
50+ 32 GB+ Dedicated server

Our Pick

Contabo at €5.99/mo gives you 4 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM — enough for a solid 15-20 player server. For larger servers, Hetzner’s CX42 (4 vCPU, 16 GB) at €13.99/mo is excellent value.

item.title: Best VPS for Small Business 2026
item.description:
Best VPS for Small Business

Small businesses need reliable hosting with good support. Here are our top picks:

Provider Starting Price Best For Support
DigitalOcean $12/mo Growing businesses Knowledge base, tickets
Linode $12/mo Beginners Documentation, community
Cloudways $11/mo Managed hosting 24/7 live chat

🥇 DigitalOcean — Best Overall

$12/mo gets you 1 vCPU, 2 GB RAM, and 60 GB storage. The excellent documentation means you can self-serve most issues.

🥈 Cloudways — Best Managed Option

If you don’t want to manage the server yourself, Cloudways is the best managed VPS for small businesses. Starts at $11/mo and includes 24/7 support.

item.title: Best VPS for WordPress 2026
item.description:
Best VPS for WordPress

WordPress needs a server that can handle PHP processing and database queries without slowing down. Here are the best picks:

Provider Starting Price Why for WordPress
DigitalOcean $12/mo One-click WordPress, great docs
Vultr $6/mo 32 locations, fast global performance
Hetzner €6.99/mo Best performance for heavy WP sites
Linode $12/mo StackScripts for automated WP setup

Recommended Minimum Specs

Traffic Level vCPU RAM Storage Monthly Budget
Low (< 5k visits) 1 2 GB 50 GB $6-12/mo
Medium (5-20k) 2 4 GB 80 GB $12-18/mo
High (20k+) 4 8 GB 160 GB $24-40/mo

WordPress Optimization Tips

  1. Use a CDN — Cloudflare (free) caches static content globally
  2. Install a caching plugin — WP Rocket or W3 Total Cache
  3. Switch to PHP 8.x — Significantly faster than PHP 7.x
  4. Use an object cache — Redis or Memcached for database queries
  5. Image optimization — Compress images before uploading (or use Smush)

item.title: How to Choose a VPS Hosting Provider
item.description:
Choosing a VPS can feel overwhelming with dozens of providers and confusing pricing plans. Here’s a straightforward framework to narrow it down.

Step 1: Define Your Use Case

What are you building?

Use Case Recommended Specs Budget Range
Personal blog / small site 1 vCPU, 1 GB RAM $5-6/mo
WordPress / CMS 1-2 vCPU, 2-4 GB RAM $6-12/mo
Web app / API 2 vCPU, 4 GB RAM $12-18/mo
SaaS product 4 vCPU, 8+ GB RAM $20-40/mo
Minecraft / game server 4 vCPU, 8+ GB RAM $14-24/mo
AI / ML workloads 4+ vCPU, 16+ GB RAM, GPU $40+/mo

Step 2: Set Your Budget

  • Under $5/mo — Vultr ($2.50) or Hetzner (€3.99)
  • $5-10/mo — DigitalOcean or Linode
  • $10-20/mo — Hetzner CX42 or Vultr High Frequency
  • $20+/mo — Hetzner dedicated or managed VPS

Step 3: Pick Managed or Unmanaged

  • Unmanaged (cheaper, more control) — You handle setup, security, updates. Best if you know Linux.
  • Managed (easier, costs more) — The provider handles maintenance. Good if you just want to focus on your website.

Step 4: Consider Data Center Location

Choose a data center close to your audience. Most providers offer 8-32 locations. For global audiences, pick a provider with 10+ locations (Vultr, DigitalOcean).

item.title: How to Deploy WordPress on a VPS
item.description:
Deploy WordPress on a VPS in 10 Minutes

This guide works on any VPS provider. I’ll show you the fastest way using a one-click app or manual setup.

Option 1: One-Click App (2 minutes)

Most VPS providers offer a WordPress one-click image:

  1. Create a new VPS instance
  2. Select “WordPress” from the one-click apps
  3. Choose your plan and data center
  4. Deploy and grab your login URL from the server console

Works on: DigitalOcean, Vultr, Linode (varies by provider)

item.title: VPS vs Shared Hosting: Which One Do You Need?
item.description:
VPS vs Shared Hosting

This is one of the most common questions beginners ask. The answer is simpler than you think.

Key Differences

Factor Shared Hosting VPS
Resources Shared with others Dedicated to you
Price $2-10/mo $5-100+/mo
Performance Variable (noisy neighbors) Consistent
Control Limited Full root access
Setup One-click Requires configuration
Scalability Limited Easy to upgrade

When Shared Hosting is Fine

  • One small website (< 5,000 visitors/mo)
  • You don’t need custom software
  • You want everything managed for you
  • Budget is very tight

When You Need VPS

  • Your site is growing (5,000+ visitors/mo)
  • You need to install custom software
  • You want better security and isolation
  • You’re running multiple websites
  • You need root access
  • You’re building a SaaS or web app

The Short Version

If shared hosting works for you, use it. When it stops working, you’ll know — your site will slow down or crash during traffic spikes. That’s when you move to VPS.

item.title: What is VPS Hosting? A Beginner's Guide
item.description:
What is VPS Hosting?

VPS stands for Virtual Private Server. Think of it as having your own private room in a large apartment building (the physical server).

How it Works

A physical server runs multiple virtual machines using a hypervisor (VMware, KVM, Xen). Each virtual machine:

  • Has dedicated CPU, RAM, and storage allocated to it
  • Runs its own operating system (Linux or Windows)
  • Can be restarted, upgraded, or rebuilt independently
  • Is isolated from other VPS instances on the same server

VPS vs Other Hosting Types

Hosting Type Resources Control Price Best For
Shared Shared with others Minimal $2-10/mo Small blogs
VPS Dedicated virtual Full root $5-100/mo Growing sites, apps
Dedicated Server Full physical server Complete $50-500+/mo High-traffic, compliance

Common Uses for a VPS

  • Hosting websites and web applications
  • Running game servers (Minecraft, Terraria)
  • VPN or proxy server
  • Development and testing environment
  • Email server
  • Database server
  • File storage and sync

Key Benefits

  1. Scalability — Upgrade or downgrade in minutes
  2. Root access — Install any software you need
  3. Isolation — Other users’ traffic doesn’t affect you
  4. Cost-effective — Much cheaper than dedicated hardware
  5. Learning — The best way to learn Linux server administration
